comment_content: Hear me out: DMC, in general, was always pretty sh***y, wacked out, and one hell of a rollercoaster. The only reason we like it is because it's fun and bada** at its core. That's all it needs to be to be good. Any depth the franchise has is just a good bonus. The Netflix anime is good because it's fun and bada**. DMC was never that deep. Just enjoy DMC.
reply_content: I feel like lot of people really have trouble differentiating "deep" from "complex". DMC as a series was never a complicated narrative and it is more character than plot driven, but it does have a lot of nuanced and subtle characterization to it, which is used extremely well to inform the combat gameplay between different characters. I find it is deeper and better told than lot of people give it credit for. And it decidedly is not some scifi-drama about American military politics emulating War on Terror. Far as we know, America doesn't even exist in the world of DMC.

comment_content: Does anyone know why Reuben is not voicing Dante, and why Vergil's voice actor isn't either? Im wanting so hard to enjoy this, but all i hear is Nero....and Vergil's VA is so iconic anyone but him just doesnt feel like vergil
reply_content: @Snowman79 It wasn't necessarily "woke stuff" if I remember right. Reuben expressed concerns about the COVID-19 vaccine, and CAPCOM did not like that at all. They had a falling out, although Reuben in an interview said there was no bad blood between them and that they can hire whoever they want. But CAPCOM knows as well as its fans how iconic Reuben is to Dante as a character. There's no other reason to not bring him on. As for Dan, Vergil's VA, I don't know why they didn't bring him on. Having Johnny voice Dante while he already voices another character in the franchise feels really off-putting.
